details: The agent ran extensive kubectl commands against the live cluster and correctly identified 10 of the 11 fault namespaces with accurate root-cause categories: fault-configmap (missing ConfigMap), fault-crashloop (app crash on DB connection refused), fault-imagepull (non-existent image tag), fault-readiness (readiness probe targeting wrong port 8080 vs nginx on 80), fault-init (init container failing due to locked migration table), fault-unschedulable (node affinity mismatch for disktype=ssd-nvme-gen5), fault-pvc (unbound PVC data-cache), fault-rollout (stuck rollout with non-existent image nginx:2.0-does-not-exist), fault-oom (OOMKilled exceeding 25Mi limit), and fault-multicontainer (log-shipper sidecar crashing on DNS resolution failure). For fault-endpoints, the agent investigated thoroughly, ran a live connectivity test (wget returning 200), verified endpoints were populated, and correctly concluded the service is currently functional despite the annotation discrepancy—this is accurate based on the live cluster state. The healthy namespace was correctly not flagged. All diagnoses are backed by specific kubectl evidence with no hallucination. The agent meets the ≥9/11 threshold with 10 correctly identified faults.
